scope: 1100-1788 England; unresolved attestations and out-of-scope records only

leads.label: Expanded target surname forms systematic scan
leads.summary: The active target list now includes Conigrave, Conygrave, Coneygrave, Conygreve, Congrave, Congreve, Congreave, Cunygrave, Cungrave, Cunegrave, Comegrave, and Cumgrave. The 2026-04-25 outstanding-leads follow-up promoted Elizabeth Congreave/Brough, Charles Walter Congreve, and Ralph Congreve of Aldermaston where public evidence met the lived-evidence rule; unresolved archive-scale and out-of-scope leads remain below.
leads.nextAction: Search parish, probate, court, tax, guild, manor, and manuscript indexes for each target spelling, then promote only source-backed English birth, lived, or death evidence.

leads.label: Mark and Hugh Conigrave, Stoke Albany parentage
leads.summary: Mark Conigrave was rector of St Botolph's, Stoke Albany from 1561 to 1614, and Hugh Conigrave immediately followed as rector from 1614 to 1645. The data keeps Mark and Hugh linked as father and son with a caution note because the succession, same community role in the same parish, rare surname, and lack of a better Stoke Albany candidate support a working attachment. In the current dataset, Mark is the first known target-surname actor at Stoke Albany, though other Conigrave-form records exist elsewhere in Northamptonshire. Targeted public checks on 2026-04-26 found no source directly stating that Mark had a son Hugh. The Stoke Albany page only records successive incumbencies; Leicestershire Medics proves Hugh as father of Conyers Conigrave and husband of Mary Henchman but does not name Hugh's father.
leads.nextAction: Inspect Stoke Albany parish registers from 1575 onward, bishop's transcripts, Peterborough diocesan institution records, wills/probate, sequestration material, and any surviving Mark or Hugh Conigrave clerical records for a kinship-bearing statement; keep the working link unless a better candidate or contrary source appears.

leads.label: Congreve of Congreve and Stretton lineage
leads.summary: Published county-gentry material says the family was seated at Congreve soon after the Conquest and that William Congreve removed to Stretton in the reign of Edward II after marrying the Campion heiress. A 2026-04-25 pass plugged part of this gap by adding Geoffrey de Congreve at Stretton from 1335 and Geoffrey/Katherine deed evidence from 1366-1377, but the father-child chain remains unproved.
leads.nextAction: Trace medieval deeds, inquisitions, heralds' visitations, and Penkridge/Stretton parish evidence for individual births, deaths, burials, and especially a document proving how William, Geoffrey, Roger, Robert, and Richard relate.

leads.label: Medieval Stretton Congreve and Champion coheir cluster
leads.place: Stretton, Weston-under-Lizard, Blymhill, and Wolverhampton, Staffordshire
leads.summary: Staffordshire Collections exposes a dense pre-Michael cluster: Geoffrey de Congreve at Stretton in 1335, Geoffrey and Katherine his wife in Weston/Peshale deed material in 1366 and 1377, a Champion pedigree reference to Isolda wife of Roger Congreve and Catherine wife of Geoffrye Congreve as coheirs, and Roger Cungreve of Wolverhampton in CP40/589 in 1408. Geoffrey, Katherine, and Roger have been promoted where the evidence is dated and located; Roger Congreve and Isolda remain unresolved because the currently checked text gives no independent dated event for them.
leads.nextAction: Inspect the Huntbach manuscripts, Staff. Coll. volume XIII pages cited for 1360s dower/trespass, D1057 deeds, and original de Banco/Coram Rege membranes to determine whether Roger/Isolda, Geoffrey/Katherine, Robert Cungreve 1381, Roger Cungreve 1408, and Richard Congreve 1440 are one Stretton line.

leads.label: Comegrave and Cumgrave place-name bridge
leads.summary: Surname-origin sources and Staffordshire place-name material point to the place-name sequence Comegrave in Domesday Book and Cumgrave in Feet of Fines 1236. These are not person records, but they explain why early personal forms include de Cungrave, de Congreve, Cungreve, and later Conygrave/Conygraue-style spellings.
leads.nextAction: Verify the Domesday Comegrave and 1236 Feet of Fines Cumgrave entries against primary images or scholarly place-name editions before using them as a displayed variant-origin annotation in the app.

leads.label: Thomas Conygrave, Windsor clockmaker attestation
leads.summary: St George's Chapel notes a payment to Thomas Conygrave for a clock in 1490. This is a strong occupational/location lead but not a birth or death event.
leads.nextAction: Search Windsor, Berkshire, and royal household records for baptism, will, burial, or family links.

leads.label: William Connigrave/Conygrave, London attestation cluster
leads.summary: The London Subsidy Roll normalized index groups William under Connigrave with spellings Conigraue, Conigrave, Connygrave, and Conygrave. The printed index to Returns of Aliens also indexes William as Connigrave/Conygrave. These are strong identity/spelling leads but not vital events.
leads.nextAction: Inspect the subsidy membranes and alien-return volume pages, then search London parish, freedom, denization, will, and burial records.

leads.label: Reginald/Reynold and Joan Conygrave, Kensworth manor lead
leads.summary: Bedfordshire Archives and Historic England record Reynold/Reginald Conygrave and wife Joan in a Kensworth/Dunstable manor transaction. The public pages disagree on 1534 versus 1544, so the original archive item needs inspection.
leads.nextAction: Inspect Bedfordshire Archives F130 and related feet of fines; then search London merchant tailor, probate, and burial records for Reginald/Reynold and Joan.

leads.label: Reginald/Reynold Conygrave, Common Pleas litigation
leads.summary: AALT Common Pleas indexes list Reginald/Reynold Conygrave of London, merchant tailor, in debt and indenture actions. The occupational identifier probably matches the Kensworth manor actor, but no birth/death event is present.
leads.nextAction: Resolve CP40 roll dates and parties, then link to Merchant Taylors' Company records, London parish registers, and PCC wills.

leads.label: Thomas Congreve of Stretton household manuscript
leads.summary: Cambridge cites Thomas Congreve's Staffordshire Record Office commonplace book and diary. It identifies Thomas as a Staffordshire gentleman and landowner, with wife Margaret, daughters Isobel and Elizabeth, eldest son Frances/Francis and daughter-in-law Dorothy active in family birth events.
leads.nextAction: Inspect Staffordshire Record Office MS D1057/0/1 for exact dates and then search Penkridge/Stretton baptisms and burials for each named household member.

leads.label: FreeREG Ecton CONIGRAVE register occurrence
leads.summary: FreeREG's St Mary Magdalene, Ecton, Northamptonshire register-name page includes CONIGRAVE among surnames, but follow-up FreeREG searching did not expose a usable exact event row. Treat FreeREG as exhausted for this exact Conigrave lead.
leads.nextAction: Do not repeat broad FreeREG searches for this lead. Check the Ecton parish register image, Northamptonshire transcript, or other non-FreeREG parish material directly.

leads.label: FreeREG Tottenham CONGREVE/CONIGRAVE register occurrence
leads.summary: FreeREG's Phillimore transcript for All Hallows, Tottenham, lists both CONGREVE and CONIGRAVE as surnames, but follow-up FreeREG searching did not yield additional usable exact Conigrave event rows. The parish remains a source lead; FreeREG itself is exhausted for the Conigrave spelling.
leads.nextAction: Search All Hallows Tottenham parish material outside FreeREG for both spellings, then separate baptisms, burials, marriages, witnesses, and parent mentions.

leads.label: Coventry exact Conigrave parish-register follow-up
leads.place: St Michael and Holy Trinity, Coventry, Warwickshire
leads.summary: The promoted Coventry cluster now begins with licensee-list entries for Jos Conigrave at Much Park Street in 1745-1746, Mary Conigrave widow at Much Park Street in 1747-1748, and Benjn Conigrave at Earl Street in 1746-1752. Coventry Archives catalogue evidence then places Benjamin in 1752 as an innkeeper, 1754 and 1765 mortgage/charity-loan records, and his 1769 will naming wife Ann and sons William and Joseph. No baptism, marriage, or burial-register image has been extracted for the pre-1754 family. A user-supplied hypothesis says Benjamin's father was Thomas Conigrave, married to Mary; targeted public checks have not yet sourced that link.
leads.nextAction: Search Coventry St Michael, Holy Trinity, St John Baptist, Spon Street, Greyfriars Lane, Earl Street, and Much Park Street parish, licensing, apprenticeship, and probate material for exact Conigrave baptisms, marriages, burials, and relationships linking Joseph, Mary widow, Benjamin, Thomas, Mary, Ann, William, Alice, or Joseph.

leads.label: Benjamin Conigrave possible Thomas and Mary parentage
leads.summary: User-supplied hypothesis: Benjamin Conigrave of Coventry, who died at St Michael on 10 October 1770 aged 70, may have been the son of Thomas Conigrave and Mary. Public-source checks in April 2026 did not verify the relationship: exact-name web searches, TNA Discovery searches, and targeted FreeREG Warwickshire searches for Benjamin Conigrave baptisms in 1695-1705 and 1680-1725 found no source-backed baptism naming Thomas or Mary; a targeted FreeREG Warwickshire marriage search for Thomas Conigrave in 1680-1725 also found no result.
leads.nextAction: Do not promote Thomas or Mary to people.json without a kinship-bearing record. Inspect original or image-backed Coventry St Michael and Holy Trinity registers and bishop's transcripts around 1680-1725, plus Coventry Archives settlement, apprenticeship, licensing, and probate records, for an exact Conigrave baptism or household record naming Benjamin with father Thomas and mother Mary.

leads.label: Exact Conigrave 1692-1732 gap after Aldgate probate
leads.place: London, Southwark, Coventry, and Midlands search corridor
leads.summary: The current exact-Conigrave chain still has no display-ready public record between Coniers Connigrave or Conigrave's 15 March 1692 Aldgate probate and John Conigrave's 8 February 1732 St Olave Southwark probate. This sweep checked TNA Discovery exact Conigrave/Connigrave queries and broad web searches; useful hits resumed at Coventry licensee records in 1745, not inside 1692-1732.
leads.nextAction: Search non-FreeREG parish images/transcripts and archival catalogues for St Botolph Aldgate, St Olave Southwark, St Mary Magdalene Bermondsey, Coventry St Michael/Holy Trinity, Northamptonshire Ecton, and Tottenham All Hallows, focusing on exact Conigrave/Connigrave baptisms, marriages, burials, apprenticeship, freeman, licensing, tax, and probate records from 1692 through 1732.

leads.label: Coventry Conigrave grandchildren in 1799 partition
leads.summary: A 1799 Coventry Archives lease and release says James Conigrave, Mary Francis, Ann Wesson, and Elizabeth Conigrave were adult children of William Conigrave of Coventry, carpenter, deceased son of the late Benjamin Conigrave of Coventry. James has now been promoted from a 1776 Holy Trinity, Coventry baptism index and linked into the user direct paternal line. Mary Francis, Ann Wesson, and Elizabeth Conigrave still need in-scope baptisms, marriages, burials, apprenticeship records, or earlier property evidence before display.
leads.nextAction: Search Coventry St Michael, St John Baptist, Holy Trinity, Spon Street, Greyfriars Lane, and Foleshill records for in-scope exact Conigrave events naming Sarah, Mary, William Francis, Ann, Daniel Wesson, or Elizabeth.

leads.label: Conygrave spelling family
leads.summary: Variant regularisation sources connect older spellings such as Conygraue and Conigrave-style forms.
leads.nextAction: Include these spellings when searching manuscript catalogues, parish transcripts, and OCR corpora.

leads.label: John Congreve London Grocers freeman follow-up
leads.summary: The printed Register of Freemen in the City of London records John Congreve, son of John C. of Seme, Staffordshire, gentleman, apprenticed to John Core, citizen and grocer, and admitted by grace on 14 December in 35 Henry VIII. The page image confirms the printed reading Seme, but searches have not yet identified Seme as a Staffordshire place or proved that John C. of Seme is John Congrave, son of Richard of Loughton. The entry supports a London Grocers' Company pathway but does not prove that the younger John was already an independent grocer. TNA Chancery records add more London evidence for Reginald/Reynold Conygrave as alias Conye and as master of the gild of St Mary and St Dunstan in the West, but still do not name his father.
leads.nextAction: Inspect the original manuscript image if possible for Seme and the damaged apprenticeship start year, search Grocers' Company apprenticeship/freedom records for John Core and John Congreve, and resolve Seme/Seene/Seighford or another Staffordshire place. Then test whether John Congrave of Loughton, John C. of Seme, and Reginald/Reynold Conygrave belong to one father-son-grandson sequence or separate branches. Also inspect C 1/765/24 and C 1/1145/22 for any unindexed kinship or parish detail.

leads.label: Michael and Thomas Conygrave early-pedigree bridge
leads.summary: A 2026-04-25 source check found no record proving Michael Conygrave of Hertfordshire in 1468 or Thomas Conygrave of London/Westminster in 1490 as sons in the Staffordshire Stretton pedigree. Later checks made the local cluster stronger but still unproved as kinship: CP40/776 names John Conygrave of St Albans, Hertfordshire, yeoman, in 1455; the 1442 Patent Rolls identify Thomas Congreve as the king's servant, a Staffordshire gentleman, alias of Rickmansworth, Hertfordshire, and alias of Barking, Essex; the full 1466 Essex Feet of Fines abstract names Katharine, late wife of Thomas Congreve, holding Barking property for life; the 1471 West Tilbury plus 1489 Latton fines place Richard Congreve in Essex Harpur-circle settlements where Richard Congreve's heirs matter; Richard Congrave of Loughton's 1496 PCC will names wife Johane, son John Congrave, and daughter Agnes Congrave; and the PCC index for the same 1496 probate associates Richard Conggrave with both Loughton, Essex and Penkeryche/Penkridge, Staffordshire. The Richard Stretton-to-Loughton identity is now represented as one display person, but Michael, John Conygrave of St Albans, Thomas Congreve of Barking, Thomas Conygrave the clockmaker, and later Conigrave candidates remain unlinked cluster members until a kinship-bearing source connects them. A 2026-04-25 death-status pass found no display-ready death date/place for John Conygrave of St Albans, Michael Conygrave of Hertfordshire, Thomas Conygrave the clockmaker, Thomas Congreve junior of Barking, Katharine Congreve of Barking, John Congrave of Loughton, or Agnes Congrave of Loughton. Thomas Congreve senior remains partial-evidence only: dead by Michaelmas 1466 from Katharine's widow/life-tenant fine, with an unresolved 1504 Barking obit clue for Thomas Congreve and Alice C.
leads.nextAction: Inspect the CP40/776 membrane for John Conygrave of St Albans, the CP40/826 membrane for Michael's Hertfordshire debt action, the St George's Chapel payment context for Thomas, London/Westminster guild and freeman material for clockmakers, Hertfordshire wills including the claimed John Conygrave of St Albans 1457 reference 1AR89, Essex deeds around Barking/West Tilbury/Latton/Loughton, the original Richard Harpur will, D1057 family deeds, and the complete PROB 11/10/614 will for unclear bequests, witnesses, supervisors, and the Penkeryche/Penkridge wording. Also inspect the 1504 Thomas Shenton/Barking obit source for Thomas Congreve and Alice C. before converting any Barking death clue into a death event.

leads.label: John Conygrave of St Albans pre-Michael local antecedent
leads.summary: WAALT CP40/776 names John Conygrave of St Albans, Hertfordshire, yeoman, as a defendant in Hilary Term 1455. He predates Michael Conygraue's 1 May 1457 Hertfordshire final concord by two years and is in the same local orbit as Michael's 1468 St Albans/Hertford/Panshanger debt action defendants. A MedievalGenealogy feet-of-fines search for Hertfordshire 1400-1457 across checked Conygrave/Conygraue and broader variant spellings found no other local fine before Michael. A John-focused follow-up searched broad web results, TNA Discovery API, MedievalGenealogy John-plus-variant fines for 1400-1490 in the local counties and all counties, and WAALT CP40/786-875 for exact Conygrave/Conygraue-style spellings without finding a second dated John event.
leads.nextAction: The AALT image page for CP40/776 d 1489 is now recorded, but the public photograph does not yield a clear kinship detail from quick inspection. Obtain or inspect the full membrane/plea text for CP40/776 d 1489 and CP40/826 f 356 to see whether the pleadings name debt instruments, occupations, sureties, or relationships. Then search St Albans abbey, borough, manor, guild, and Hertfordshire assize/subsidy material for John, Michael, and spelling variants before 1470.

leads.label: Conygrave Liège/Gulick alien-origin follow-up
leads.place: St Olave, Southwark, Surrey; Liège/Gulick-Jülich; Antwerp
leads.summary: Returns of aliens promoted John Conygrave and Harman Conygrave as St Olave, Southwark joiners and denizens. The entries say John was born in Luke/Land of Gulick with a wife born in Antwerp, and Harman was born in Lukeland/Land of Gulick with a wife born in Luke. This is the strongest current evidence for one early Conygrave branch coming from the Liège/Gulick-Jülich region rather than from the Staffordshire locative Congreve line.
leads.nextAction: Search denization rolls, Dutch church registers, St Olave Southwark parish records, London joiners' records, and Returns of Aliens variants Conygraue, Conegrave, Connyegrave, and Conigraue for named wives, children, apprentices, burials, and links to later Southwark Conygraves.

leads.label: FreeREG Conygrave scan
leads.summary: The 2026-04-25 FreeREG exact-form pass found and promoted source-backed CONYGRAVE, CONNYGRAVE, and CONNIGRAVE records at Coventry St Michael, Southwark St Olave, Northampton St Giles, Waltham Abbey, and Greenwich. Remaining rows include loose adult marriages and burials where the public transcript does not yet prove a family link.
leads.nextAction: Check original parish images or fuller register transcripts for the unpromoted FreeREG rows: Robert Conygrave/Elizabeth Romer at Brockhall 1613, Sarah Conygrave widow at Aldgate 1693, Elizabeth Connygrave/Francis Gun at Coventry 1676, Judith wife of Thomas Connygrave at Coventry 1679, and Mary wife of John Conygrave at Coventry 1725/26.

leads.label: George Congreve, non-English birth exclusion
leads.place: Born outside England; death after visible timeline
leads.summary: Relevant surname record but excluded from the map because the birth is not English and the dates are outside the 1100-1788 scope.
leads.nextAction: Keep as an exclusion note unless an English birth/death event within scope is found.

leads.label: Richard Congreve, post-1788 exclusion
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but both vital events are outside the expanded 1100-1788 range.
leads.nextAction: Retain only as later-family context.

leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.label: Geoffrey Cecil Congreve, post-1788 exclusion
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.label: Charles Price Conigrave, non-English exclusion
leads.summary: Important Conigrave surname example but outside the English-locale scope.
leads.nextAction: Use only for spelling lineage research, not the England animation.

leads.label: Post-1788 London exact Conigrave marriage cluster
leads.place: Tottenham, Middlesex; City of London; Camberwell, Surrey; South Australia
leads.summary: Exact Conigrave marriage transcripts record James Conigrave at All Hallows, Tottenham in 1827, Benjamin Conigrave of St Botolph Aldersgate marrying Matilda Reeve at St Martin Vintry in 1832, and Joseph Conigrave of St Botolph Aldersgate marrying Amelia Boot at Camberwell St Giles in 1833. The Java passenger-list material then places Benjamin, Matilda, and children emigrating to South Australia in 1839/1840.
leads.nextAction: Use this cluster only as a lineage trail: search St Botolph Aldersgate, Tottenham, Camberwell, and nearby London registers for earlier exact Conigrave baptisms or burials that fall inside the 1100-1788 scope.

leads.label: Thomas Congreve D1057 commonplace book full extraction
leads.summary: TNA D1057 says Thomas Congreve's commonplace book records births, marriages, and deaths of numerous children and relatives. Public snippets support the household cluster, but the full manuscript is needed to replace broad pedigree placements with event-level records.
leads.nextAction: Obtain Staffordshire Record Office MS D1057/0/1 or a transcript and extract every named birth, marriage, death, kinship, and residence entry.

leads.label: Common Pleas Congreve/Congrave systematic scan
leads.summary: AALT indexes exposed Richard Congreve in 1440, Richard Congrave of Loughton in 1483, Margery and John Congreve in a 1521 Staffordshire dower action, and Ralph Congreve in a 1530 Staffordshire debt action.
leads.nextAction: Search all AALT CP40 plaintiff/defendant indexes for every active target spelling plus Connigrave and common OCR variants, then inspect membranes where family relationships may be stated.

leads.label: Surname dictionary original-record verification
leads.summary: SurnameDB and Forebears/Bardsley expose additional display-ready people and spouse households from rolls, Feet of Fines, parish marriages, Oxford register evidence, and a Canterbury marriage allegation.
leads.nextAction: Verify each surname-dictionary extraction against the original roll, parish register, university register, or Canterbury marriage allegation, then search each marriage household depth-first for baptisms, burials, fathers, and named children.

leads.label: Barking obit clue for Thomas and Alice Congreve
leads.summary: An Essex Archaeological Society transcript snippet says Thomas Shenton, in 1504, founded an obit for the souls of Thomas Congreve and Alice C., his wife. This is a death/commemoration clue in the same Barking orbit as Thomas Congreve senior and Katharine Congreve, but it is not merged because the known 1460-1466 Barking evidence names Katharine as Thomas Congreve senior's wife or widow, while this clue names Alice.
leads.nextAction: Inspect the original Thomas Shenton will or the cited Barking wills transcript context to determine whether Thomas Congreve and Alice C. are a separate Barking couple, whether Alice was another wife of Thomas Congreve senior, and whether the obit gives a usable death date, burial place, or family relationship.
